sections.heading: Complications
sections.content:
Hypocalcemia (transient) after total thyroidectomy: 5.4% (moderate)
Permanent hypocalcemia: 1.1% (severe)
Recurrent laryngeal nerve injury: 3.4% (severe)
Symptoms of upper aerodigestive tract compression or invasion (medullary carcinoma): up to 15% (severe)
Fatigue (dabrafenib/trametinib): 38% (mild)
Pyrexia (dabrafenib/trametinib): 37% (moderate)
Nausea (dabrafenib/trametinib): 35% (mild)

labels: Symptoms of upper aerodigestive tract compression or invasion (medullary carcinoma)

cards.name: Hypocalcemia (transient) after total thyroidectomy
cards.detail: Hypoparathyroidism: most common significant complication of thyroidectomy; transient clinical hypoparathyroidism is common in adults and children undergoing total thyroidectomy; reported hypocalcemia in 5.4% immediately after total thyroidectomy, persisting in only 0.5% at 1 year

cards.name: Recurrent laryngeal nerve injury
cards.detail: Recurrent laryngeal nerve injury: reported incidence of 3.4% for long-term injury

cards.name: Symptoms of upper aerodigestive tract compression or invasion (medullary carcinoma)
cards.detail: Symptoms reported by up to 15% of patients with sporadic MTC. Surgical resection is the main treatment for MTC. In the presence of grossly invasive disease, more extended procedures with resection of involved neck structures may be appropriate.

cards.name: Fatigue (dabrafenib/trametinib)
cards.detail: Dabrafenib/trametinib: common adverse events include fatigue (38%), pyrexia (37%), and nausea (35%)

cards.name: Pyrexia (dabrafenib/trametinib)
cards.detail: Dabrafenib/trametinib: common adverse events include fatigue (38%), pyrexia (37%), and nausea (35%)

cards.name: Nausea (dabrafenib/trametinib)
cards.detail: Dabrafenib/trametinib: common adverse events include fatigue (38%), pyrexia (37%), and nausea (35%)
